## business_overview (rendered report body)

*Auto-generated from structured data because the writer output failed validation (Editorial summary length must be 120-320 words (current: 22).).*
## Executive Summary
Walmart Delivers 4.7% Revenue Growth to $713.2B in FY26, Driven by Omnichannel and eCommerce Strength Management's disclosed tone was positive. Total revenues increased 4.7% year-over-year to $713.2 billion, driven by strong positive comparable sales across U.S. segments and international markets.; Consolidated net income rose to $22.3 billion, up from $20.2 billion in FY25, with diluted EPS reaching $2.73.; Operating cash flow grew to $41.6 billion, supporting $26.6 billion in capital expenditures and generating $14.9 billion in free cash flow.; Board of Directors approved a new $30.0 billion share repurchase authorization in February 2026.

## Financials
- Total Revenues: $713.2B vs. $681.0B (+4.7%) – Driven by increases in average ticket, transactions, and unit volumes, alongside strength in eCommerce.
- Consolidated Net Income: $22.3B vs. $20.2B (+10.5%) – Benefited from higher operating income and net increases in the fair value of equity and other investments.
- Diluted EPS: $2.73 vs. $2.41 (+13.3%) – Reflects strong net income growth and ongoing share repurchases.
- Operating Cash Flow: $41.6B vs. $36.4B (+14.1%) – Increase primarily due to higher cash provided by operating income and lower cash tax payments.
- Profitability: Gross profit rate increased 8 basis points to 24.2%, driven by disciplined inventory management in the U.S. and growth in higher margin businesses globally.; Operating expenses as a percentage of net sales increased 20 basis points to 20.9%, impacted by a $0.9B increase in self-insured general liability claims and a $0.7B PhonePe share-based compensation charge.
- Cash flow: Generated $41.6B in operating cash flow, up from $36.4B in FY25.; Free cash flow increased to $14.9B, up from $12.7B in FY25, despite a $2.9B increase in capital expenditures to support omnichannel growth.
- Balance sheet: Total assets increased to $284.7B as of January 31, 2026, up from $260.8B in the prior year.; Return on Investment (ROI) slightly decreased to 15.1% from 15.5% in FY25, primarily due to an increase in average invested capital from higher property and equipment purchases.

## Risks
- Cybersecurity threats and potential data breaches (Evidence: Despite our security measures, however, there can be no assurance that we, or the third parties with which we interact, will not experience a cybersecurity incident in the future that will materially affect us.)
- Ongoing legal proceedings and regulatory investigations (Evidence: The Company is involved in legal proceedings arising in the normal course of its business, including litigation, arbitration and other claims... including Opioid-Related Litigation, False Claims Act Litigation, and FTC Driver Platform Litigation.)
- Environmental regulatory compliance and potential penalties (Evidence: In October 2023, the Company received a Finding of Violation from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (the 'EPA') alleging violations of the Clean Air Act... the EPA could seek penalties in excess of $1 million.)

## Management Commentary
- Themes: Omnichannel expansion and eCommerce integration; Margin accretion through productivity improvements and business mix optimization; Disciplined capital allocation prioritizing automation, supply chain, and store remodels
- Capital allocation: Allocated $26.6B to capital expenditures in FY26, heavily focused on supply chain, customer-facing initiatives, technology, and store remodels.; Returned capital to shareholders via share repurchases, utilizing a $20.0B program and authorizing a new $30.0B program in February 2026.
> "Our objective of prioritizing growth means we will focus on serving customers and members however they want to shop through our omnichannel business model." – Management

## Outlook
- Guidance: Not disclosed—specific forward-looking quantitative guidance not provided in the excerpt.
- Tone: cautious
- Drivers: Tariffs and trade restrictions; Inflationary trends and fluctuations in global currencies; Supply chain pressures and volatility in fuel prices
- Watch items: Swings in macroeconomic conditions and their effect on consumer confidence; Changes in employment trends

### Financial Overview

• Total Revenues: $713.2B (vs. $681.0B) — +4.7% — Driven by increases in average ticket, transactions, and unit volumes, alongside strength in eCommerce.
• Consolidated Net Income: $22.3B (vs. $20.2B) — +10.5% — Benefited from higher operating income and net increases in the fair value of equity and other investments.
• Diluted EPS: $2.73 (vs. $2.41) — +13.3% — Reflects strong net income growth and ongoing share repurchases.
• Operating Cash Flow: $41.6B (vs. $36.4B) — +14.1% — Increase primarily due to higher cash provided by operating income and lower cash tax payments.

### Management Commentary

Themes: - Omnichannel expansion and eCommerce integration
- Margin accretion through productivity improvements and business mix optimization
- Disciplined capital allocation prioritizing automation, supply chain, and store remodels
Quotes: - Speaker: Management
Quote: Our objective of prioritizing growth means we will focus on serving customers and members however they want to shop through our omnichannel business model.
Context: Discussing the company's strategic focus on integrating physical and digital retail experiences.
Capital Allocation: - Allocated $26.6B to capital expenditures in FY26, heavily focused on supply chain, customer-facing initiatives, technology, and store remodels.
- Returned capital to shareholders via share repurchases, utilizing a $20.0B program and authorizing a new $30.0B program in February 2026.
